cost_of_revenue.calculation_note: Cost of revenue — Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income, page 5

operating_expenses.calculation_note: Sum of Research and development (17,699) + Marketing and sales (2,908) + General and administrative (2,614) = 23,221 — Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income, page 5. Cost of revenue excluded.

cash_and_equivalents.calculation_note: Cash and cash equivalents — Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets, page 6

short_term_debt.calculation_note: No current portion of long-term debt or short-term borrowings identified on the Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets, page 6. Operating lease liabilities (current) excluded.

long_term_debt.calculation_note: Long-term debt — Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets, page 6

net_debt.calculation_note: Short-term debt (0) + Long-term debt (58,748) - Cash & Cash Equivalents (23,426) = Net debt (35,322) — Balance Sheet data from page 6

capital_expenditure.calculation_note: Purchases of property and equipment — Condensed Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows, page 7

free_cash_flow.calculation_note: Free cash flow — Reconciliation of GAAP to Non-GAAP Results, page 9

shares_outstanding.calculation_note: Total shares outstanding at period end date not explicitly stated in the report. Only weighted-average share counts are provided.

shares_weighted_average_diluted.calculation_note: Weighted-average shares used to compute earnings per share — Diluted — Condensed Consolidated Statements of Income, page 5

sections_found.summary: Total revenue grew 33% year-over-year to $56.31 billion, driven by a 19% increase in ad impressions and a 12% rise in average price per ad. On a constant currency basis, revenue growth was 29%, reflecting a favorable foreign exchange tailwind.

Total costs and expenses increased 35% year-over-year to $33.44 billion, with R&D being the largest component at $17.70 billion. Despite the faster expense growth, operating margin held steady at 41% year-over-year.

Net income rose 61% to $26.77 billion, significantly boosted by an $8.03 billion income tax benefit related to U.S. Treasury Notice 2026-7 addressing Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax treatment of capitalized R&D costs. Excluding this benefit, diluted EPS would have been $3.13 lower.

Family of Apps generated $55.91 billion in revenue and $26.90 billion in operating income, while Reality Labs contributed $402 million in revenue but posted an operating loss of $4.03 billion. Reality Labs losses narrowed slightly compared to Q1 2025.

Capital expenditures including principal payments on finance leases totaled $19.84 billion in Q1 2026, reflecting significant ongoing investment in infrastructure. Full-year 2026 capex guidance was raised to $125–145 billion from the prior range of $115–135 billion, driven by higher component pricing and additional data center costs.

Operating cash flow was $32.23 billion, and free cash flow was $12.39 billion after subtracting property purchases and finance lease payments. Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities stood at $81.18 billion as of March 31, 2026.

Family daily active people averaged 3.56 billion in March 2026, up 4% year-over-year, though slightly down quarter-over-quarter due to internet disruptions in Iran and WhatsApp restrictions in Russia.

Q2 2026 revenue is guided at $58–61 billion with an approximately 2% foreign currency tailwind. Full-year 2026 total expenses are expected in the $162–169 billion range, and the tax rate for remaining 2026 quarters is expected to be 13–16% absent tax landscape changes.

Meta flagged ongoing legal and regulatory headwinds in the EU and U.S., including youth-safety scrutiny and scheduled U.S. trials that could result in material losses. Broader risks include macroeconomic conditions, geopolitical events, competition, and dependence on advertising revenue.
